Pews became common during this 16th c. religious upheaval; sermons got longer & people literally could no longer stand for them

[IMAGE CLUE]
Colonial families often sat together in box pews, like the ones seen here in this Massachusetts church where Paul Revere saw the light
This main part of a church where the pews are located takes its name from the Latin for "ship", which it's kinda shaped like

[IMAGE CLUE]
These Quaker places of worship are known for simplicity; seating is plain chairs or benches often facing each other
This egg-shaped building's original pews--enough to seat nearly 9,000--were made of pine from the Wasatch Range of the Rocky Mountains
It's a mechanical device with rotating blades to move a ship or aircraft
Per Bart Simpson, Heywood U. Cuddleme might be a good this, a fictitious sobriquet used to conceal your identity
A music website bears the name of this tool used for lifting hay
It's a seismic event in the Sea of Tranquility, for example
In ancient Athens Cleon, who urged brutal measures against the city of Mytilene, was this type of suspect populist leader
Gay Talese wrote a classic 1966 magazine story about what happens when this singer "Has a Cold" (he's not in a good mood)
Louis "Dr. No" Ignarro won a Nobel for work on this medical molecule; it's NO, not to be confused with N2O or "laughing gas"

Of Italian heritage, she was Florida's first female attorney general & became the USA's AG in 2025

She immigrated to the U.S. with her husband in the 1950s, & her cookbooks made her almost synonymous with Italian food

questions.clue: Sabrina Carpenter's first No. 1 hooks up with an early Beatles hit to make a really, really, really, really earnest request

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ginnifer Goodwin presents the clue.) It doesn't take a detective to know when a rabbit is happy or excited; just watch for one of these next-level bunny hops--a high-twisting leap that shares its name with a word for a baby's pacifier

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Bateman presents the clue.) Foxes have great night vision & great hearing; plus, some have claws that do this, meaning to withdraw, which enables them to climb trees

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ginnifer Goodwin presents the clue.) Good luck at the DMV in Zootopia, where the employees are sloths, which are so slow; in fact, sloths have the lowest this of all non-hibernating mammals, meaning their bodies turn food into energy verrryy slowwwly

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Ke Huy Quan presents the clue.) Since lynxes are powerful predators, it's no surprise the Lynxleys are one of the most influential families in Zootopia; North America has Canadian lynxes & these furry cousins sometimes called bay lynxes

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Bateman presents the clue.) In "Zootopia 2", we meet Nibbles Maplestick, the beaver; known as ecosystem engineers, beavers can alter their environments by building dams, canals & these dome-shaped dwellings that can be up to 16 feet tall

final.clue: This spin-off premiered 21 years after the show that inspired it but took place nearly a century later
